repoState.title: Repo state is healthy
repoState.detail: The repo-local control surfaces are present, validation is clean, and the desktop app can safely mirror the CLI view of repo state.
repoState.sourceOfTruthNote: Repo-local artifacts under .agent/ and promoted repo instruction files remain the source of truth. The desktop app only reads and reports that state.

continuity.label: Current focus
continuity.value: Review .agent/context/context-tree.json, then record a checkpoint before non-trivial implementation or handoff.

available.name: Android Specialist
available.purpose: Kotlin, Gradle, and Android app implementation or review with repo-local build and test discipline.

available.name: Architecture Specialist
available.purpose: Planning, decomposition, boundary analysis, and guarded change sequencing.

available.name: Backend Specialist
available.purpose: API, service, persistence, and contract-safe backend delivery across Python, Node, and general server-side repos.

available.name: Data Platform Specialist
available.purpose: Safe work on pipelines, transforms, schemas, warehouse logic, and migration-sensitive data-platform repos.

available.name: Docs Specialist
available.purpose: Improve docs, onboarding, summaries, and repo knowledge flow without drifting from authority.

available.name: Frontend Specialist
available.purpose: UI and interaction work with emphasis on safe boundaries, browser evidence, and repo-aware frontend guidance.

available.name: Fullstack Specialist
available.purpose: Balanced implementation specialist for app and web repos that cross frontend, backend, routing, and validation boundaries.

available.name: iOS Specialist
available.purpose: Swift, SwiftUI, Xcode, and Apple-platform implementation or review with honest tool-availability boundaries.

available.name: MCP Specialist
available.purpose: Safe capability selection for MCP-aware workflows without assuming live secrets or machine-local authority.

available.name: Push Specialist
available.purpose: Conservative push-readiness and review gating before commit or push decisions.

available.name: Python Specialist
available.purpose: Safe Python and backend implementation follow-through with explicit boundaries and validation.

available.name: QA Specialist
available.purpose: Validation, edge-case coverage, and acceptance confidence for repo-local changes.

available.name: Refactor Specialist
available.purpose: Keep larger restructures bounded, contract-safe, and reviewable.

available.name: Release Specialist
available.purpose: Release readiness, checkpoint health, and phase-close discipline before commit or push decisions.

available.name: Review Specialist
available.purpose: General software engineering review for correctness, regression risk, contract safety, and change quality.

available.name: Security Specialist
available.purpose: Review auth, permissions, data exposure, and unsafe assumptions without weakening guardrails.

activeProfile.name: Fullstack Specialist
activeProfile.purpose: Balanced implementation specialist for app and web repos that cross frontend, backend, routing, and validation boundaries.

recommendedProfile.name: Fullstack Specialist
recommendedProfile.purpose: Balanced implementation specialist for app and web repos that cross frontend, backend, routing, and validation boundaries.

specialists.safeParallelHint: Keep generic repos quiet. Fan out only when file ownership and reconcile responsibilities are explicit.

selectedPack.name: Research Pack
selectedPack.description: Planning and documentation pack for discovery, migration research, and architecture work.
guidance: Use for planning, docs, migration prep, and cross-repo research tasks.
Prefer read-heavy tools and keep repo-local outputs compact and inspectable.
realismNotes: This pack is runtime-selectable and prefers research-oriented integrations when they are registered.
Search availability still depends on the active repo profile and installed integrations.

suggestedPack.name: Research Pack
suggestedPack.description: Planning and documentation pack for discovery, migration research, and architecture work.
guidance: Use for planning, docs, migration prep, and cross-repo research tasks.
Prefer read-heavy tools and keep repo-local outputs compact and inspectable.
realismNotes: This pack is runtime-selectable and prefers research-oriented integrations when they are registered.
Search availability still depends on the active repo profile and installed integrations.

available.name: Core Pack
available.description: Default repo-first pack for filesystem, git-aware reasoning, and contract inspection.
guidance: Use for any repo that only needs baseline repo-state reading, routing, and contract maintenance.
Prefer this pack when the repo is generic and should stay quiet by default.
realismNotes: This pack is runtime-selectable and narrows Kiwi to a baseline repo-first integration lane.
Actual capability availability still depends on the active repo profile and installed integrations.

available.name: Research Pack
available.description: Planning and documentation pack for discovery, migration research, and architecture work.
guidance: Use for planning, docs, migration prep, and cross-repo research tasks.
Prefer read-heavy tools and keep repo-local outputs compact and inspectable.
realismNotes: This pack is runtime-selectable and prefers research-oriented integrations when they are registered.
Search availability still depends on the active repo profile and installed integrations.

available.name: Web QA Pack
available.description: Browser and verification guidance for web apps, UI review, and smoke testing.
guidance: Use when Playwright or browser automation helps validate UI, routing, and regressions.
Keep browser verification additive. Never imply that UI automation is universally available.
realismNotes: This pack is runtime-selectable and prefers browser and design validation integrations when they are registered.
Playwright-style automation still depends on the active repo profile and installed integrations.

available.name: AWS Pack
available.description: AWS-specific routing and safety guidance for repos that genuinely target AWS.
guidance: Only recommend this pack for AWS repos or tasks that clearly require AWS services.
Prefer repo-local guidance and existing SSO/account conventions over ad hoc credential handling.
realismNotes: This pack stays blocked until AWS-specific integrations are registered in Kiwi's repo registry and machine inventory.
Do not imply AWS tooling exists here until the pack reports executable.
available.unavailablePackReason: AWS Pack is blocked because this repo does not register any AWS-specific MCP or tool integrations in the current registry/inventory.

available.name: iOS Pack
available.description: Apple-platform guidance for Xcode-aware workflows, simulator checks, and UI validation.
guidance: Recommend for iOS and macOS repos when Xcode or simulator tooling is available.
Keep repo-local runbooks authoritative and treat bridge tooling as optional acceleration.
realismNotes: This pack stays blocked until Xcode or simulator integrations are registered in Kiwi's repo registry and machine inventory.
Do not imply Apple-platform bridge support exists here until the pack reports executable.
available.unavailablePackReason: iOS Pack is blocked because this repo does not register any Xcode or simulator integrations in the current registry/inventory.

available.name: Android Pack
available.description: Android-focused guidance that leans on repo-local docs, Gradle, and emulator workflows.
guidance: Recommend for Android repos, especially when Gradle, Kotlin, and emulator checks are relevant.
Default to repo-local guidance and normal build/test tooling before claiming special bridge support.
realismNotes: This pack stays blocked until Android-specific integrations are registered in Kiwi's repo registry and machine inventory.
Do not imply emulator or Android bridge support exists here until the pack reports executable.
available.unavailablePackReason: Android Pack is blocked because this repo does not register any Android-specific MCP or emulator integrations in the current registry/inventory.

compatibleCapabilities.purpose: neural web and code search for current research, docs, and examples
usageGuidance: Use for current research, library examples, or code references when repo-local authority is insufficient.
antiPatterns: Do not use it to outrank trusted repo authority.
Do not fetch secrets or rely on uncontrolled external snippets.

compatibleCapabilities.purpose: targeted web search and scraping for current site-specific information
usageGuidance: Use for focused site search or extraction when official docs or repo context are not enough.
antiPatterns: Do not treat scraped content as trusted without source review.
Do not use it when simpler local context already answers the question.

compatibleCapabilities.purpose: general web search for current information and discovery
usageGuidance: Use for broad discovery when you do not yet know the best source.
antiPatterns: Do not stop at generic search results when authoritative sources exist.

usageGuidance: Use when packets need vendor or framework documentation that should outrank generic memory.
Prefer this for specialist packets that need authoritative references.
antiPatterns: Do not treat it as a substitute for repo-local authority files.
Do not use it to infer secrets or hidden machine-local state.

usageGuidance: Prefer for planner, reviewer, and release-check reasoning where a traceable chain is helpful.
antiPatterns: Do not overuse it for trivial inline edits.
Do not let reasoning traces replace concrete validation evidence.

usageGuidance: Use for PR, CI, and repository metadata when repo-local context alone is insufficient.
antiPatterns: Do not assume PR state replaces local diff review.
Do not trigger write-capable operations without an explicit user request.

mcpPacks.note: Research Pack is active via heuristic default. 6 MCP integration(s) match this pack policy.

tools.name: GitHub MCP
tools.description: Repository, issue, pull request, and workflow access through a structured MCP surface.

tools.name: Notion MCP
tools.description: Structured access to Notion workspaces and knowledge pages.

tools.name: Supabase MCP
tools.description: Project-aware MCP access for Supabase resources and operational context.

tools.name: Awesome Agent Skills
tools.description: Curated catalog of reusable `SKILL.md`-style capability packages across platforms.

tools.name: Awesome Copilot Skills
tools.description: Collection of skills, hooks, plugins, and workflows for Copilot-style agent experiences.

workflows.name: Agentic Workflows
workflows.description: Markdown-defined AI workflows and automations from the Awesome Copilot ecosystem.

workflows.name: Plugin Marketplace Flow
workflows.description: Marketplace-driven plugin and MCP discovery model from the Claude Code ecosystem.

workflows.name: Progressive Disclosure Skills
workflows.description: Skill discovery model that loads lightweight metadata before full instructions.

capabilities.name: Repos / Issues / PRs
capabilities.description: Structured repo and pull request context for decision support.

capabilities.name: Jira / Confluence
capabilities.description: Linked planning and documentation surfaces through external MCP integrations.

capabilities.name: Task-triggered skills
capabilities.description: Intent-based skill discovery with metadata-first loading and optional deeper instructions.

capabilities.name: Hook-style lifecycle actions
capabilities.description: Event-driven workflow hooks that inform execution and verification without replacing the runtime.

notes: This catalog is advisory only. Kiwi Control does not execute these tools directly.
The catalog is sourced from permissively licensed, read-only ecosystem lists and is intended to inform decisions, not drive runtime behavior automatically.

machineAdvisory.note: Machine-local advisory only. Optimization scores are heuristic completeness estimates calculated from inspected machine signals and never override repo-local Kiwi state. Machine-local advisory fast mode is active for desktop repo loading.

machineParity.boundaryNote: Repo-local Kiwi capability is built into the product. Machine-global parity measures optional external setup that Kiwi can benefit from, but does not treat as canonical repo truth.

feedback.note: Adaptive feedback is idle. Kiwi Control learns only from successful checkpoints and handoffs.

indexing.coverageNote: Run kiwi-control prepare to record indexing coverage and selection reasoning.
